atoms.content: Descriptive statistics summarize and organize characteristics of a dataset. They don't predict the future; they just describe what the data currently looks like.
Measures of central tendency include the Mean (average), Median (middle value), and Mode (most frequent value). They locate the center of your data.
Variance and Standard Deviation measure how spread out the data is. High standard deviation means data points are far from the average.
Descriptive stats are like the scoreboard at a basketball game. It tells you the current score and player fouls, but doesn't predict who will win.
If Jeff Bezos walks into a bar, the 'average' wealth skyrockets. Which metric should you use instead to find typical wealth? (Answer: Median)
Range is the simplest measure of spread: maximum value minus minimum value. It's sensitive to outliers.
Percentiles divide data into 100 equal parts. The IQR (Interquartile Range) spans the 25th to 75th percentile and is used to detect outliers.
Skewness measures asymmetry in your data distribution. Positive skew has a long right tail; negative skew has a long left tail. Many ML algorithms assume low skew.
